root_text.an3.71:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an3.71:1.2: Atha kho channo paribbājako yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā ānandena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an3.71:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho channo paribbājako āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.71:1.4: “tumhepi, āvuso ānanda, rāgassa pahānaṁ paññāpetha, dosassa pahānaṁ paññāpetha, mohassa pahānaṁ paññāpethā”ti.
root_text.an3.71:1.5: “Mayaṁ kho, āvuso, rāgassa pahānaṁ paññāpema, dosassa pahānaṁ paññāpema, mohassa pahānaṁ paññapemā”ti.
root_text.an3.71:2.1: “Kiṁ pana tumhe, āvuso, rāge ādīnavaṁ disvā rāgassa pahānaṁ paññāpetha, kiṁ dose ādīnavaṁ disvā dosassa pahānaṁ paññāpetha, kiṁ mohe ādīnavaṁ disvā mohassa pahānaṁ paññāpethā”ti?
root_text.an3.71:3.1: “Ratto kho, āvuso, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti;
root_text.an3.71:3.2: rāge pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.71:3.3: Ratto kho, āvuso, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati;
root_text.an3.71:3.4: rāge pahīne neva kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, na vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, na manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.71:3.5: Ratto kho, āvuso, rāgena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti;
root_text.an3.71:3.6: rāge pahīne attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.71:3.7: Rāgo kho, āvuso, andhakaraṇo acakkhukaraṇo aññāṇakaraṇo paññānirodhiko vighātapakkhiko anibbānasaṁvattaniko.
root_text.an3.71:4.2: mūḷho kho, āvuso, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attabyābādhāyapi ceteti, parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti;
root_text.an3.71:4.3: mohe pahīne nevattabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na parabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na ubhayabyābādhāyapi ceteti, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an3.71:4.4: Mūḷho kho, āvuso, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, manasā duccaritaṁ carati;
root_text.an3.71:4.5: mohe pahīne neva kāyena duccaritaṁ carati, na vācāya duccaritaṁ carati, na manasā duccaritaṁ carati.
root_text.an3.71:4.6: Mūḷho kho, āvuso, mohena abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ nappajānāti;
root_text.an3.71:4.7: mohe pahīne attatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, paratthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti, ubhayatthampi yathābhūtaṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an3.71:4.8: Moho kho, āvuso, andhakaraṇo acakkhukaraṇo aññāṇakaraṇo paññānirodhiko vighātapakkhiko anibbānasaṁvattaniko.
root_text.an3.71:4.9: Idaṁ kho mayaṁ, āvuso, rāge ādīnavaṁ disvā rāgassa pahānaṁ paññāpema.
root_text.an3.71:4.10: Idaṁ dose ādīnavaṁ disvā dosassa pahānaṁ paññāpema.
root_text.an3.71:4.11: Idaṁ mohe ādīnavaṁ disvā mohassa pahānaṁ paññāpemā”ti.
root_text.an3.71:5.1: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.an3.71:5.2: “Atthāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.71:5.3: “Katamo panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti?
root_text.an3.71:5.6: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.71:5.7: “Bhaddako kho, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā etassa rāgassa dosassa mohassa pahānāya.

translation_text.an3.71:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an3.71:1.2: Then the wanderer Channa went up to Venerable Ānanda and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an3.71:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to Ānanda:
translation_text.an3.71:1.4: “Reverend Ānanda, do you advocate giving up greed, hate, and delusion?”
translation_text.an3.71:2.1: “But what drawbacks have you seen, Reverend Ānanda, that you advocate giving up greed, hate, and delusion?”
translation_text.an3.71:3.1: “A greedy person, overcome by greed, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.71:3.2: When greed has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.71:3.3: A greedy person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.71:3.4: When greed has been given up, they don’t do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.71:3.5: A greedy person doesn’t truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.71:3.6: When greed has been given up, they truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.71:3.7: Greed is a destroyer of sight, vision, and knowledge. It blocks wisdom, it’s on the side of distress, and it doesn’t lead to extinguishment.
translation_text.an3.71:4.1: A hateful person, overcome by hate, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. …
translation_text.an3.71:4.2: A deluded person, overcome by delusion, intends to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.71:4.3: When delusion has been given up, they don’t intend to hurt themselves, hurt others, and hurt both. They don’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an3.71:4.4: A deluded person does bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.71:4.5: When delusion has been given up, they don’t do bad things by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.71:4.6: A deluded person doesn’t truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.71:4.7: When delusion has been given up, they truly understand what’s for their own good, the good of another, or the good of both.
translation_text.an3.71:4.8: Delusion is a destroyer of sight, vision, and knowledge; it blocks wisdom, it’s on the side of distress, and it doesn’t lead to extinguishment.
translation_text.an3.71:4.9: This is the drawback we’ve seen in greed, hate, and delusion, and this is why we advocate giving them up.”
translation_text.an3.71:5.1: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion?”
translation_text.an3.71:5.2: “There is, reverend, a path and a practice for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion.”
translation_text.an3.71:5.4: “It is simply this noble eightfold path, that is:
translation_text.an3.71:5.5: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.an3.71:5.6: This is the path, this is the practice for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion.”
translation_text.an3.71:5.7: “This is a fine path, a fine practice, for giving up that greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.an3.71:5.8: Just this much is enough for diligence.”
